title: Fix container filters
body:
container filters were being double encoded (maybe triple) which resulted in the wrong encoding representation of filters being sent by the go-bindings.  Also, on the server side, Filter needed to be changed to Filter to decode properly. Finally, due to the changed return type of List Containers, the go bindings return values needed to be changed.

Signed-off-by: Brent Baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *baude* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [baude] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment

Ok, I tested this and now it parses the filters, which is awesome!
However it is still a bit difficult to use:
`libpod/containers/json {all: true, filter: "id=<container_id>"}` returns all containers, ignoring the filter. Fair enough, even though doc says `Return all containers. By default, only running containers are shown` which implies to me, it is there to just be able to query all, not just running containers.

So when I drop `all:true`:
`libpod/containers/json {filter: "id=<container_id>"}` and the container is running, it returns only this one. __However__ when the container is not running, I get just `null`. I know there is also `status=(created|restarting|running|removing|paused|exited|dead)` but I would not prefer to pass this whole thing. (not sure even if possible to declare more statuses at once).

I think it should work like this:
```py
containers = allRuningContainers() # Start with just running containers
if all:
    containers = allContainers() # If all passed, assume all containers
if filter:
    containers = containers.filter(filter) # Then apply filters on containers that you have prepared
return containers
```

--- notes ----
```

INFO[0153] convertUrlValuesString: Failed to Unmarshal id=d02eae7e32877625198c4f7e5cfb1404cfd00b38c035f57f4bbc322fc04a2652: invalid character 'i' looking for beginning of value
```
